other_observers: husband, Gordon Beatty; at our feeders, yes.
specific_location: approximately 5 miles south of Sedalia, and 5 miles west of Castle Rock, Christy Ridge subdivision, along C-105.
description: slightly smaller than house finch, and paler red primarily in forehead wing bars deeply forked tail  black chin paler reddish breast streaked sidesBehaviors: I noticed them (6) at my feeder Christmas day, absentmindedly thinking they were goldfinches, because of the wingbars. then decided to take a closer look, and could see the reddish "hat", smallish size, and forked tail! they were on the thistle feeder, primarily.Call: finch-like, with trillsPlumage: winter
